knownIssues.title: Windows Mixed Reality was deprecated by Microsoft in 2024
knownIssues.description: Microsoft deprecated the WMR platform in November 2024. The runtime still works on existing installs but receives no updates. The Lenovo Explorer is a first-generation WMR headset and should be considered fully end-of-life.
knownIssues.workaround: WMR continues to function on systems where it is already installed. New PC setups require downloading the WMR installer from archive sources. This is a legacy device with no ongoing software development.

knownIssues.title: Two-camera inside-out tracking has a narrow tracking volume
knownIssues.description: The Explorer uses two forward-facing cameras for inside-out tracking. Controllers held below the waist, behind the body, or far to the sides frequently lose tracking.
knownIssues.workaround: Keep controllers within the forward field of view. Avoid throwing motions or reaching behind the body. Games requiring wide controller movements are not well suited to this headset.

knownIssues.title: Halo-style strap is not adjustable mid-session
knownIssues.description: The Lenovo Explorer's halo strap does not have a quick-tighten mechanism. Adjusting fit requires stopping and manually resizing, which can be inconvenient.
knownIssues.workaround: Set strap tightness before starting a session. Add foam padding to the head rest area if slippage is an issue during active games.

optimizationTips.tip: The Explorer is GPU-friendly — even mid-range GPUs run it well
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: The Explorer's lower resolution means most modern GPUs can handle it easily without needing to reduce quality settings.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: 1440x1440 per eye at 90Hz has modest GPU requirements by modern standards. GTX 1070-class hardware maintains 90fps in most titles at 100% SteamVR SS. This makes the Explorer good for testing VR setups or as an entry-level device. Set SteamVR to 100-120% SS for visible sharpness improvement.

optimizationTips.tip: Supersampling to 120-140% significantly improves sharpness on this headset
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: Turning up the picture quality makes a big difference on the Explorer because its screen resolution is lower — your GPU can likely handle it easily.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: At 1440x1440 per eye, aliasing is visible in distant objects and text. Supersampling at 130% SS in SteamVR provides a clear clarity boost and is well within the render budget of GTX 1080-class GPUs. Set per-application in SteamVR → Application Settings.

optimizationTips.tip: The Explorer flips up for quick real-world glances
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: Lift the visor section up when you need to look at the real world — you don't need to take the whole headset off.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: The Lenovo Explorer's visor physically flips upward, allowing the wearer to look at the physical world without removing the headset. Useful for consulting notes, taking drinks, or checking your space. Pause SteamVR or use SteamVR dashboard before flipping to avoid tracking loss errors.
